author: Авакумов Данил Эдуардович
objective: Empirically validate that emergent role weights correspond to causal contribution of each role to the task outcome.
principle_under_test: Role weight in cooperative evolution is proportional to causal influence on final task success.

formal_hypothesis.statement: Emergent role weights match normalized causal contribution measured via counterfactual role interventions.

where.C_r: expected fitness drop when role r is removed or randomized

key_findings: Mutual information does not explain role weights.
Architecture likely depends on causal control of outcome.

analysis_pipeline.title: ROLE INTERVENTION DATA COLLECTION
analysis_pipeline.goal: Run counterfactual interventions by ablating or randomizing role outputs.

analysis_pipeline.title: CAUSAL CONTRIBUTION ESTIMATION
analysis_pipeline.goal: Compute causal contribution of each role using delta fitness and Shapley estimation.

analysis_pipeline.title: NORMALIZED CAUSAL WEIGHTS
analysis_pipeline.goal: Normalize causal contributions to produce theoretical role weights.

analysis_pipeline.title: EMPIRICAL VS CAUSAL COMPARISON
analysis_pipeline.goal: Compare emergent role ratios with causal weights.

analysis_pipeline.title: LAW VALIDATION
analysis_pipeline.goal: Validate whether causal law explains cooperative architecture.

analysis_pipeline.title: FINAL REPORT
analysis_pipeline.goal: Produce formal report validating or rejecting causal role law.

definition_of_done: Empirical role ratios compared to causal predictions.
